milestone: Zero-Holder Scenes -- close the legacy fallback gap in renderer/slideRenderer.js's _resolveBorder
slice: A follow-up triggered by a live user report: a real World Builder Scene with zero Places still showed a Holder placeholder box in Creator. Traced to renderer/slideRenderer.js (a Studio file, not a Builder file, but recorded here too since it's the render path every Builder-authored Theme flows through): the earlier zero-Holder gating only ever blocked _resolveBorder's Artwork-Theme-specific branch, leaving the function free to fall through to the older, artwork-independent Sprint 8.4.2 'Picture Holder defaults' fallback, which can still resolve a non-null border from a theme's generic styling defaults with no relationship to Places at all -- and once non-null, the artwork placeholder box still drew regardless of the Scene's real Holder count. Fixed by gating _resolveBorder at its very top instead of inside one branch, so zero-Holder now uniformly means no border resolves, matching what Working View/Runtime Preview already show for a Scene with no Places. Verified via a real Playwright A/B test (git stash-toggling the fix, comparing full-panel-rect pixel diffs on a synthetic zero-Holder Scene compiled through the real ProjectCompiler.runBuild pipeline) confirming the fix is a real, disclosed gap-closure; it did not reproduce the user's own exact symptom against this synthetic fixture, so that report is still being triaged separately. Full regression across all 18 existing Creator/Builder Playwright scripts and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged.
